jokes.text: PBS. The propaganda wing of Bill and Melinda Gates, and viewers like you.
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically calling PBS propaganda while using their actual tagline
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical take on PBS funding that subverts their familiar tagline with conspiracy theory overtones. Well-structured and economical.

jokes.text: It will be nice to see everyone again. I haven't seen Kevin since we let him go.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of firing someone as 'letting him go'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jim's awkward relationship with management decisions, delivered with typical understatement.

jokes.text: I've been looking forward to this day since I was 18 years old.
jokes.punchline: Implying he's hated work his entire adult life
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley line - completely in character, revealing his lifelong work misery with typical bluntness.

jokes.text: Speech! No.
jokes.punchline: Stanley's immediate flat refusal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy - two words capture Stanley's entire personality. Perfect character integration.

jokes.text: Get out? What does that mean? / It's a colloquial way of saying you're fired, Kevin.
jokes.punchline: Dwight's overly formal explanation of being fired
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/payoff with Kevin's obliviousness contrasting Dwight's pretentious formality. Creative firing method.

jokes.text: The cake has spoken, Pam. Sorry.
jokes.setup: Dwight needs justification for firing Kevin
jokes.punchline: Treating cake frosting as divine judgment
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Dwight logic - treating confectionery as infallible authority. Highly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: Toby will stop it. Any time anyone's ever been fired, Toby's blocked it, so... / Yeah. Yeah, I don't think... / Toby, wait, wait. Hold that thought. Here's your cake.
jokes.setup: Pam expecting Toby to save Kevin as usual
jokes.punchline: Dwight immediately fires Toby mid-sentence with another cake
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - just as Pam mentions Toby always blocking firings, he gets fired himself. Perfect comic timing and structure.

jokes.text: Bye, bye, Toby
jokes.punchline: Dwight's sing-song farewell
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Dwight's glee at finally firing Toby, delivered in his characteristic style.

jokes.text: At least I got chocolate.
jokes.punchline: Finding the tiny silver lining in his cake flavor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Toby response - pathetically optimistic even while being fired. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: I bike to work now. It saves on gas, it's cheaper than a vasectomy
jokes.setup: Jim listing practical benefits of biking
jokes.punchline: Comparing transportation to surgical sterilization
jokes.explanation: Unexpected comparison creates humor through absurd juxtaposition. Slight callback to show's vasectomy running gag.

jokes.text: My heart is so open. I'm so at peace. / Ugh. Look at Meredith. She's disgusting.
jokes.setup: Angela claiming inner peace and openness
jokes.punchline: Immediately judging Meredith harshly
jokes.explanation: Perfect character hypocrisy - Angela's pretend enlightenment instantly undermined by her judgmental nature.

jokes.text: Those feet. They're like the paws of an orangutan.
jokes.punchline: Specific animal comparison for feet
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Angela's specific brand of cruelty, though the insult itself is fairly standard.

jokes.text: Now that she's wearing sports bras, we don't see her boobs as much.
jokes.setup: Angela finding something positive about Meredith
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about containing her breasts
jokes.explanation: Typical Angela - even her compliments are insulting. Good character consistency but predictable content.

jokes.text: Oh, and the old man to feed us the cheese that he's been fermenting since the day of my birth.
jokes.setup: Dwight listing who needs to be at wedding
jokes.punchline: Bizarre wedding tradition involving decades-old cheese
jokes.explanation: Perfectly bizarre Schrute tradition - specific, disgusting, and completely in character. Creates vivid mental image.

jokes.text: I hired him back after Creed faked his own death in the baler the day after the doc aired.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of Creed's elaborate fake death scheme
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - starts mundane then reveals Creed's characteristically insane response to documentary fame.

jokes.text: The only person he fooled was Kevin.
jokes.setup: Creed's fake death in industrial equipment
jokes.punchline: Only Kevin was dumb enough to believe it
jokes.explanation: Good character callback - even elaborate death hoax only fools Kevin. Economical and character-consistent.

jokes.text: Turns out Creed was in the band The Grass Roots, in the 1960s. During that time, the police say he sold drugs. And trafficked in endangered species meat. And stole weapons-grade LSD from the military.
jokes.setup: Revealing Creed's past in a famous band
jokes.punchline: Each crime escalates in absurdity and severity
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure with each revelation more outrageous. Completely fits Creed's mysterious criminal past.

jokes.text: Party time, whorehouse. / No. No whorehouse. This is Dwight's night. Okay?
jokes.punchline: Jim having to explicitly veto prostitution
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe humor - Andy's inappropriate suggestion met by Jim's responsible response. Predictable but functional.

jokes.text: bestisch Mensch
jokes.punchline: Dwight's made-up German compound word
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Dwight's pretentious fake German traditions. Fits his character perfectly.

jokes.text: Guten pranken.
jokes.setup: Jim planning good surprises for Dwight
jokes.punchline: Jim's fake German for 'good pranking'
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to Jim's prank history while mimicking Dwight's fake German. Economical and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: often times in Hollywood portrayals of bachelor parties, there are accidental murders. That won't be necessary tonight.
jokes.setup: Dwight warning about bachelor party dangers
jokes.punchline: Treating movie tropes as real concerns and dismissing murder as 'unnecessary'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dwight logic - taking movie clichés seriously while casually dismissing murder. Perfect character voice.

jokes.text: Mose has been weird? That's so unlike him.
jokes.setup: Dwight saying Mose is acting weird
jokes.punchline: Jim sarcastically noting Mose's normal weirdness
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response from Jim, highlighting the absurdity of calling Mose weird as if it's unusual.

jokes.text: when I started sobbing uncontrollably, apparently, that struck a chord with quite a lot of people. Not a very compassionate chord.
jokes.punchline: Musical pun about striking the wrong kind of chord
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay connecting musical terminology to emotional response. Good structure and self-aware humor.

jokes.text: You can't just sit here and cry. / I can so just sit here and cry!
jokes.punchline: His defiant response that became a meme
jokes.explanation: The line that went viral - petulant and childish in a way that's both pathetic and memorable.

jokes.text: I actually got a call from the Double Rainbow guy and the fat Star Wars kid. Turns out they have a support group. Not really my scene.
jokes.setup: Andy connecting with other viral video stars
jokes.punchline: Rejecting support group for viral fame victims as beneath him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy arrogance - even among fellow viral failures, he thinks he's above them. Good cultural references.

jokes.text: He used to call it a 'keleven.' He told Dwight, 'A mistake plus keleven gets you home by 7:00.' He was home by 4:45 that day.
jokes.punchline: Kevin's made-up number and flawed logic that somehow worked
jokes.explanation: Instant classic - 'keleven' is perfectly Kevin, combining mathematical incompetence with accidental success. Incredibly quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: I live in Poland now. The Scranton of the EU.
jokes.setup: Packer describing his new life abroad
jokes.punchline: Comparing Poland to Scranton as equally undesirable
jokes.explanation: Sharp geographic comparison that insults both places. Economical and fits Packer's crude worldview.

jokes.text: How long have you been stalking me? / No, no, no. My flight just got in from New York. / Are you still with Pyotr? / No, and I thought I unfriended you.
jokes.punchline: Kelly's failed attempt to cut contact through social media
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing their unchanged dynamic - Kelly's desperation vs Ryan's manipulation.

jokes.text: I have six roommates, which are better than friends, you know, because they have to give you one month's notice before they leave.
jokes.punchline: Treating legal requirements as friendship benefits
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin logic - finding upside in transactional relationships. Shows his fundamental misunderstanding of friendship.

jokes.text: Hey-o!
jokes.punchline: The familiar Kevin catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Standard Kevin callback - not particularly funny on its own but provides character consistency and recognition.

jokes.text: A bear hug from my favorite mama grizzly.
jokes.setup: Andy getting a hug from Phyllis
jokes.punchline: Calling her a mama grizzly bear
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay connecting bear hugs to mama bears, though fairly predictable. Fits Andy's tendency toward elaborate descriptions.

jokes.text: Must've been another devilishly handsome debonair individual.
jokes.setup: Someone thinking they saw Darryl at the airport
jokes.punchline: Darryl's over-the-top self-description
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Darryl's confidence, though the self-aggrandizement is somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: You remember my two Lap-Band surgeries, right? / Neither do I.
jokes.setup: Pam mentioning medical procedures as excuses
jokes.punchline: Admitting the surgeries were fictional
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff structure revealing Pam's creative excuse-making. Self-aware and economical.

jokes.text: Guten prank number one.
jokes.punchline: Numbering his 'good pranks' in fake German
jokes.explanation: Running gag setup - establishes the structure for Jim's planned surprises using established fake German.

jokes.text: They wanted me to go to the bachelorette party with the girls. Really? Such a cliché. I'm a man.
jokes.punchline: Rejecting gender stereotypes while embracing others
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Oscar's intellectual pretension mixed with traditional masculinity concepts.

jokes.text: Waazzuup!
jokes.setup: Oscar trying to act traditionally masculine
jokes.punchline: Using outdated beer commercial reference
jokes.explanation: Shows Oscar's awkward attempt at 'straight guy' behavior using dated pop culture reference. Character-appropriate awkwardness.

jokes.text: What, are you going to whack me, Jim? / No, Dwight. You'll be doing the whacking.
jokes.punchline: Jim revealing it's for Dwight's enjoyment
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - turns Dwight's paranoia into excitement. Nice setup for the paintball reveal.

jokes.text: A bazooka. You remembered.
jokes.punchline: Dwight's joy at getting military-style weapon
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Dwight's childlike joy at weaponry. Shows Jim's understanding of what Dwight actually wants.

jokes.text: Guten prank number two.
jokes.punchline: Second numbered 'good prank'
jokes.explanation: Continuation of running gag - functional but not particularly funny on its own.

jokes.text: Hey, I know you. You going to sit here and cry?
jokes.punchline: Stranger mocking his viral moment
jokes.explanation: Shows the downside of Andy's fame - public ridicule. Creates appropriate cringe discomfort.

jokes.text: We're very close. We even have our own special language. / People love it. / They do.
jokes.setup: Meredith claiming close relationship with Angela
jokes.punchline: Angela's reluctant confirmation of obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Angela forced to play along with Meredith's delusion. Shows both characters well.

jokes.text: So we'll have an onion loaf for the table, please. And tell us about your heartiest soups.
jokes.punchline: Dwight treating stripper as actual waitress
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight obliviousness - completely missing sexual context and focusing on food. Creates great cringe comedy.

jokes.text: It tastes like cigarettes. That won't work.
jokes.punchline: Dwight's practical food criticism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - sexual advance interpreted as food service. Dwight's earnest food critique is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Mom? / Wait, Wait. What? / Oh, man.
jokes.punchline: Discovering the stripper is Meredith's son
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - awkward stripper situation becomes family drama. Perfect cringe comedy through unexpected connection.

jokes.text: Give them a good show, my little entrepreneur.
jokes.setup: Meredith discovering her son is stripping
jokes.punchline: Encouraging him to continue as 'entrepreneurship'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Meredith response - treating inappropriate job as business venture. Shows her unconventional mothering style.

jokes.text: Stripper's only as good as his song.
jokes.punchline: Professional stripper critique
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Meredith's unexpected expertise in stripping industry. Perfectly fits her character.

jokes.text: Be gentle, Jakey, gentle! / If anything, this is rougher!
jokes.punchline: Angela's distressed observation about increased intensity
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - motherly advice making situation worse. Angela's horrified observation adds to the cringe.

jokes.text: You're telling me! If you want her to leave, just tip her. / What for? We haven't even gotten bread yet!
jokes.setup: Jim trying to explain stripper protocol
jokes.punchline: Dwight still focused on restaurant service
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - Dwight's complete confusion about context while maintaining restaurant expectations. Perfect obliviousness.

jokes.text: Great, which one is that? / Mmm. Hey, driver, why don't you take us to 3030 Adams?
jokes.setup: Looking for pub Dwight would never visit
jokes.punchline: Pam knowing exactly which one - Kevin's bar
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the Kevin reveal - Pam's knowledge hints at the connection without giving it away completely.

jokes.text: That's six 'wells'. Did I get that number right, Dwight?
jokes.punchline: Referencing his math incompetence while showing he can count simple things
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin comeback - self-aware about his math issues while demonstrating basic counting ability. Great character growth.

jokes.text: I heard you bought a bar, Kevin. / Yes, I did. This one. / Now, get out.
jokes.punchline: Kevin's power play as bar owner
jokes.explanation: Great character reversal - Kevin finally has power over Dwight. The table-turning is satisfying and economically delivered.

jokes.text: You? You did this as a prank. My own bestisch Mensch. / Nope, not a prank.
jokes.setup: Dwight assuming Jim's typical prank behavior
jokes.punchline: Jim denying it's a prank - it's genuine reconciliation
jokes.explanation: Good character development moment - Jim subverting expectations by being genuinely caring instead of pranking.

jokes.text: Waste of a good hatchet.
jokes.setup: Jim suggesting burying the hatchet metaphorically
jokes.punchline: Dwight taking it literally and being practical
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight literal interpretation - taking metaphor practically while showing his tool obsession. Economical and character-perfect.

jokes.text: I saw him making his portrait out of a Wooly Willy.
jokes.punchline: Using children's magnetic hair toy for portrait
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual image - Dwight's touching but bizarre way of remembering Kevin. Perfect blend of sweet and absurd.

jokes.text: Your Internet searches were so filthy we had to throw out your computer.
jokes.punchline: Computer contamination beyond repair
jokes.explanation: Great escalation in the list of Kevin's failures - treating digital content as physically contaminating. Perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Guten prank number three.
jokes.punchline: Continuing the numbered prank structure
jokes.explanation: Continuation of running gag - marks the structure but isn't particularly funny on its own.

jokes.text: And her legs are still numb from being in the trunk.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Angela can't come to pre-wedding gathering
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of kidnapping aftermath
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy - treating serious physical consequences casually. Shows Dwight's matter-of-fact attitude about violence.

jokes.text: The man who delivered my divorce papers came by fan boat, which was kind of fun.
jokes.punchline: Finding joy in the delivery method of bad news
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley observation - finding the one positive aspect in divorce proceedings. Great character voice and unique detail.

jokes.text: I got hated on pretty hard when that auto-tune went viral.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining his reception at the reunion
jokes.punchline: Reference to his auto-tuned breakdown
jokes.explanation: Standard callback to established plot point. Functional but not particularly inventive.

jokes.text: Yeah, people hate you.
jokes.setup: Andy's complicated feelings about his fame
jokes.punchline: Kevin's blunt confirmation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin bluntness - cutting through Andy's self-pity with brutal honesty. Maximum impact with minimal words.

jokes.text: It's like seeing a documentary about how your food is made. It's kind of disgusting. You learn a lot, but I didn't want to know any of it.
jokes.setup: Question about seeing their lives on TV
jokes.punchline: Food production analogy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant analogy - comparing self-awareness to food production knowledge. Original, structured well, and universally relatable.

jokes.text: Uh, no one recognizes me, but now all my friends call me Plop. So, thanks, PBS.
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic thanks for unwanted nickname
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing the documentary's unintended consequences. Nice sarcastic delivery.

jokes.text: All I can say is, if I had Jim, he'd have a free pass to do anything. I mean if I lucked into that, he could do anything. Anything.
jokes.setup: Kelly commenting on Jim and Pam's relationship
jokes.punchline: Escalating thirstiness for Jim
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kelly moment - inappropriate oversharing and Jim obsession. Creates appropriate cringe while staying in character.

jokes.text: Do you find that your life feels pointless now that nobody's actually filming you anymore? / Yes.
jokes.setup: Deep existential question about post-documentary life
jokes.punchline: Creed's immediate honest agreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed response - brutally honest about existential emptiness. Maximum comedic impact with minimal dialogue.

jokes.text: For the first seven years I was getting my PhD in school psychology and they didn't show it. Yes, I was getting hammered, but, hey, it was college.
jokes.punchline: Admitting to alcoholism while defending academic achievement
jokes.explanation: Great character revelation - Meredith's intelligence hidden behind alcoholism. Nice misdirection defending drinking as normal college behavior.

jokes.text: It's like a long book that you never want to end, and you're fine with that because you just never, ever want to leave it. / Like Harry Potter? / Yeah. Like Harry Potter.
jokes.setup: Pam's poetic description of their relationship
jokes.punchline: Andy reducing it to pop culture reference and Pam's resigned agreement
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of romantic sentiment - Andy's literal interpretation forces Pam to accept simplified version. Nice structure.

jokes.text: More just like, 'Mom, I hate you,' and then she would say, 'Go to your room, young lady,' and I'd stamp my foot and run upstairs. And I have a room, which is really cool.
jokes.setup: Erin describing potential relationship with birth mother
jokes.punchline: Getting excited about having a room in fantasy scenario
jokes.explanation: Perfect Erin moment - finding joy in basic childhood elements she missed. Touching and funny simultaneously.

jokes.text: Mom?
jokes.setup: Erin's emotional question about her father
jokes.punchline: Joan Cusack revealed as her birth mother in audience
jokes.explanation: Perfect emotional payoff - single word recognition creating huge moment. Excellent structure and genuine surprise.

jokes.text: I can show you my social security card if it helps.
jokes.punchline: Offering extreme documentation for simple introduction
jokes.explanation: Good minor character moment - social awkwardness through over-documentation. Creates mild cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Can you imagine if I'd worn my Jimmy Choos? / I just saved you 600 bucks, mister.
jokes.setup: Kelly complaining about wedding venue mud
jokes.punchline: Ryan's practical financial observation
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic - Kelly's materialism met by Ryan's cheap practicality. Shows their relationship patterns.

jokes.text: Never came back. Oldest story in the book.
jokes.punchline: Treating abandonment as cliché
jokes.explanation: Good Ryan voice - treating personal tragedy as boring cliché. Shows his emotional detachment and cynicism.

jokes.text: No, Kelly, he is not named after a hip-hop artist from 2011. It's Drake, like a mix of Drew and Blake.
jokes.punchline: Ryan's pretentious alternative explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ryan pretension - denying obvious pop culture reference for fake sophisticated reasoning. Very in character.

jokes.text: Ravi's a pediatrician, and some of his patients are total uggos. / Uh, they're called premature, sweetie.
jokes.punchline: Ravi's professional correction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cringe moment - Kelly's callous language corrected by medical professional. Perfect contrast of perspectives.

jokes.text: Thanks for not locking the door when I asked you to, Phyllis! / Sorry, Phyllis. You didn't know.
jokes.punchline: Angela immediately apologizing to herself for blaming Phyllis
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Angela's internal struggle between anger and politeness played out verbally. Shows character growth.

jokes.text: The minister just told me that it's tradition for the bestisch Mensch to be older than the groom. / Oh, come on. I've never heard of such a thing.
jokes.punchline: Dwight's skepticism of his own culture
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Michael's entrance - Jim uses Dwight's cultural obsession against him while Dwight shows rare skepticism.

jokes.text: That's what she said.
jokes.punchline: His immediate use of signature catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Pure fan service - not particularly clever but perfectly timed for Michael's return. Huge impact for character consistency.

jokes.text: Best prank ever.
jokes.punchline: Dwight recognizing it as Jim's ultimate prank
jokes.explanation: Perfect character recognition - Dwight appreciates the ultimate positive prank. Good callback to their relationship.

jokes.text: See, I get what they're trying to do, but why are the graves so shallow?
jokes.punchline: Michael's practical criticism of grave depth
jokes.explanation: Great Michael observation - understanding concept but focusing on practical execution flaw. Very in character.

jokes.text: Complimentary hay hooks are placed along the aisles. Just stab them on in there.
jokes.punchline: Casual violence in hay bale seating instructions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight hosting - treating sharp implements casually while being helpful. Shows his unique blend of violence and hospitality.

jokes.text: I let him suck on a strawberry. He's allergic, but he'll get over it fast.
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of causing allergic reaction
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Ryan moment - terrible parenting presented casually. Perfect character consistency showing his selfishness.

jokes.text: Michael has so many pictures of his kids, he had to get two phones with two numbers, and he pays two bills. He's just so happy to have a family plan.
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'family plan' meaning both phone service and life goals
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment with clever wordplay. Shows Michael's joy while creating humor through double meaning.

jokes.text: It's me.
jokes.punchline: Proudly presenting self-portrait carving
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley moment - proud of simple achievement with characteristic understatement. Maximum economy of words.

jokes.text: Kelly was hoping that you would keep the baby so they can start a new life together.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of child abandonment
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy through matter-of-fact delivery of terrible parenting. Shows Kelly and Ryan's complete selfishness.

jokes.text: I feel like all my kids grew up and then they married each other. It's every parent's dream!
jokes.setup: Michael describing his feelings about the office
jokes.punchline: Treating intra-office relationships as incestuous family dynamics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael logic - sweet sentiment made creepy through family metaphor. Perfectly captures his misguided paternal feelings.

jokes.text: It sounds kind of lame. No offense.
jokes.setup: Ryan dismissing the documentary crew's party
jokes.punchline: Adding 'no offense' to obvious insult
jokes.explanation: Standard Ryan rudeness - insults delivery with fake politeness. In character but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Well, you bought the house without telling me, so I thought I could sell it without telling you.
jokes.setup: Jim questioning Pam's secret house selling
jokes.punchline: Pam's logical retaliation reasoning
jokes.explanation: Good callback with perfect logic - Pam using Jim's own behavior to justify hers. Shows relationship growth.

jokes.text: I had to pledge $50 to my local PBS station just to get this.
jokes.punchline: Admitting to paying for party access
jokes.explanation: Good Oscar character moment - his social climbing meets PBS funding reality. Nice callback to opening PBS joke.

jokes.text: Oh, um, I kind of meant just everybody from the office.
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly excluding documentary crew and others
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Pam's well-intentioned exclusion creates social awkwardness. Shows the documentary divide.

jokes.text: It took me so long to do so many important things. It's just hard to accept that I spent so many years being less happy than I could have been.
jokes.setup: Pam reflecting on watching the documentary
jokes.punchline: Realizing her own wasted time
jokes.explanation: Touching character moment with universal appeal - regret about missed opportunities. Shows real growth and self-awareness.

jokes.text: if you film anybody long enough, they're going to do something stupid. It's only human natural.
jokes.punchline: Malapropism of 'human nature' as 'human natural'
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight moment - trying to be profound but making verbal error. Shows his attempted wisdom with typical linguistic mishap.

jokes.text: Don't be an idiot. It's for the severance.
jokes.setup: Dwight firing Jim and Pam harshly
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's to help them financially
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight character growth - mean-seeming action revealed as generous. Shows his evolution while maintaining his voice.

jokes.text: Oh, right, for what? The art? The music? The incredible night life? No, thank you.
jokes.setup: Jim inviting Dwight to visit Austin
jokes.punchline: Dwight sarcastically listing Austin's attractions while rejecting them
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Dwight's cultural dismissal through sarcastic listing. Shows his rural preferences and stubbornness.

jokes.text: Oh, I can so just sit here and cry!
jokes.punchline: Crowd chanting his viral phrase
jokes.explanation: Full circle moment - Andy's embarrassment becomes triumph. Good character arc completion through callback.

jokes.text: I wish there was a way to know you're in the good old days before you've actually left them.
jokes.setup: Andy reflecting on his time at Dunder Mifflin
jokes.punchline: Profound observation about appreciating present moments
jokes.explanation: Exceptional line - profound, universal truth delivered by unlikely character. Perfect series reflection and incredibly quotable.

jokes.text: But seriously, you made a nine-year documentary and you couldn't once show me doing my origami?
jokes.setup: Kevin's talking head about the documentary
jokes.punchline: Complaint about omitted talent
jokes.explanation: Good Kevin moment - showing pride in unexpected skill while missing bigger picture. Meta commentary on editing choices.

jokes.text: I think I'm gay. / Why do you say that? / It's just that I'm so emotional. / You're not gay. You're not gay.
jokes.punchline: Kevin's flawed logic about sexuality
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Kevin's simplistic thinking while Oscar provides patient correction. Shows both characters well.

jokes.text: I just feel lucky that I got a chance to share my crummy story with anyone out there who thinks they're the only one to take a dump in a paper shredder. You're not alone, sister.
jokes.setup: Meredith reflecting on sharing her story
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific solidarity offer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Meredith moment - touching sentiment ruined by disgusting specificity. Perfect character voice and highly quotable.

jokes.text: There's a lot of beauty in ordinary things. Isn't that kind of the point?
jokes.setup: Pam explaining why they were good documentary subjects
jokes.punchline: The show's entire thesis statement
jokes.explanation: Perfect series ending sentiment - meta-commentary delivered naturally by appropriate character. Captures show's entire philosophy.
